title: Noccaea pauciflora
taxonomy: Noccaea pauciflora is classified within the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This family is a large group of flowering plants that also includes species of cabbages, mustards, and broccoli. Noccaea pauciflora is further classified within the genus Noccaea, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Northern Hemisphere. The genus Noccaea is often divided into several subgenera based on characteristics such as leaf shape and flower arrangement. Noccaea pauciflora is also sometimes classified as Thlaspi pauciflorum, as some sources group this species under the genus Thlaspi.

intro: Noccaea pauciflora, also known as White Alpine Pennycress, is a low-growing, alpine perennial plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its purported health benefits, including its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Noccaea pauciflora include glucosinolates, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anticancer properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often marketed as dietary supplements to support overall health and well-being.
